name: Cohort pacing and capacity planning for the Bonn residential unit
consequences: Capacity constraints limit data yield, while safety and regulatory requirements demand predictable staffing. Effective pacing can sustain eight active suites and improve data capture, but the approach must include escalation protocols as occupancy approaches safety thresholds to maintain quality and safety.
options: Implement rolling admission with overlapping cohorts and modular staffing to sustain eight active suites while testing higher throughput without expanding footprint.
Adopt adaptive stay lengths guided by interim event yield, reallocating nights to higher-yield participants to maximize data capture.
Cross-train night-shift staff and implement on-call scheduling to flexibly cover demand and enable temporary capacity expansion without new hires.
review: Core tension: capacity constraints versus data yield; safety and regulatory requirements demand predictable staffing. Weakness: none of the options address explicit escalation triggers or quality-control protocols as occupancy nears safety limits.

name: Sensing stack integrity and privacy-preserving data handling
consequences: Enhanced data fidelity across streams improves event detection and phenotyping but increases operational complexity and privacy risk. A robust privacy framework enables external collaboration later while maintaining local control over sensitive data.
options: Standardize the tiered sensing with scheduled PSG nights and robust artifact rejection pipelines to improve event fidelity while keeping patient burden manageable.
Introduce automated real-time data quality checks and alerting to flag sensor dropout or artifact contamination before analysis.
Incorporate privacy-preserving data handling by storing de-identified streams locally with strict access controls and separate pipelines for sensitive video data.
review: Core tension: data richness vs privacy and burden. Weakness: options omit cross-device interoperability challenges and an auditable data governance trail across streams.

name: Recruitment and retention optimization for extended stays
consequences: Streamlined recruitment reduces time-to-enrollment and improves retention during multi-week stays, with downstream gains in data yield and analysis power. However, broader enrollment pathways may introduce variability in event prevalence and require careful screening to maintain data quality.
options: Formalize regional referral networks and streamlined pre-admission screening to accelerate enrollment and minimize attrition across the intake funnel.
Provide flexible scheduling, transport assistance, and enhanced on-site amenities to improve participant adherence across multi-week stays.
Engage patient advocacy and community outreach while enforcing privacy-preserving consent processes to expand awareness without compromising data governance.
review: Core tension: enrollment velocity versus data quality and participant burden. Weakness: options risk selection bias if criteria broaden or pre-screening becomes inconsistent across sites.

name: Data governance and privacy-by-design for residential parasomnia research
consequences: Clear governance reduces risk of data misuse and eases external collaboration by establishing verifiable consent and de-identification standards. Trade-offs include potential limits on data granularity and longer setup times for approvals across repositories.
options: Implement layered consent with tiered data sharing and de-identification protocols, plus data-use agreements with external repositories.
Establish a formal data access review process and role-based access controls to minimize exposure and ensure auditability.
Explore synthetic data generation and federated analysis options to enable external collaboration while safeguarding participant privacy.
review: Core tension: privacy guarantees reduce data utility; weakness: misses ongoing consent management and revocation as data-sharing scope evolves.

name: Funding diversification and collaboration strategy
consequences: A diversified funding plan mitigates single-source risk and supports core operations through milestones while enabling strategic partnerships. Trade-offs include negotiating publication terms and maintaining independence across partners.
options: Pursue milestone-based grant applications coupled with internal Bonn funding to ensure core operations stay funded through critical milestones.
Negotiate a structured pharmaceutical partnership with predefined publication windows while preserving independent interpretation of results.
Develop a multi-institution consortium for shared data standards and joint pilot grants to broaden funding opportunities.
review: Core tension: short-term funding milestones versus long-term independence. Weakness: lacks explicit conflict resolution and publication governance across partners.

name: Governance cadence, risk thresholds, and external oversight
consequences: Regular oversight aligns progress with safety and regulatory norms, enabling timely course corrections. However, excessive cadence can slow execution and create administrative load if responses lag behind findings.
options: Institute a quarterly risk dashboard with predefined thresholds and rapid escalation pathways to the advisory board.
Pilot staggered governance reviews with independent internal auditors and rotating chairs to avoid centralization and improve accountability.
Implement milestone-based budget reallocations with transparent documentation to maintain agility while preserving accountability across evolving project phases.
review: Core tension: speed of progress versus robust oversight. Weakness: lacks explicit remediation timelines or action plans following advisory feedback.

name: Tiered sensing architecture and escalation policy for parasomnia events
consequences: Adopting a tiered sensing architecture concentrates data collection on high-signal nights, improving resolution where it matters. It also lowers ongoing PSG exposure for participants, but adds hardware and maintenance complexity that could hinder rapid scale-up if escalation frequency remains high.
options: Adopt nightly low-burden monitoring across all participants, with automated flagging and scheduled escalation nights only when flagged events occur.
Pair continuous low-burden sensing with random mid-point full PSG checks to calibrate event detection without blanket full-night monitoring.
Centralize data review in a rolling two-tier system where technicians handle preliminary scoring and only escalate to PSG after cross-site consensus.
review: Core tension: maximizing data depth while minimizing burden and cost; Weakness: escalation protocols may over-prioritize flagged events, leaving quiet nights under-characterized and biasing prevalence estimates.

name: Participant retention through stay-environment design and engagement scheduling
consequences: Environment design and engagement scheduling can improve participant comfort and reduce dropout, increasing data yield over extended stays. The trade-off is a potential drift from strictly naturalistic sleep conditions and greater operational burden to manage activities and privacy controls.
options: Offer flexible stay schedules with tiered compensation tied to milestone engagement, while preserving safety and study integrity.
Implement a rotating activity calendar and partner check-ins to reduce fatigue and improve data yield during extended stays.
Introduce optional family-friendly accommodations and privacy controls to improve comfort without compromising study protocol compliance.
review: Core tension: balancing participant comfort with data integrity and protocol fidelity; Weakness: enhanced engagement strategies may introduce behavioral modifications that affect sleep patterns and event rates.

name: Independent adjudication workflow optimization for parasomnia events
consequences: A structured adjudication workflow improves scoring consistency and study credibility. However, external or multi-site adjudication can introduce delays and higher costs, potentially slowing timely analysis.
options: Institute weekly inter-rater calibration meetings with predefined scoring rubrics and periodic blind re-annotated samples.
Implement double-blind scoring where two scorers independently annotate events, followed by adjudication meetings if disagreements exceed a threshold.
Outsource independent scoring to an external expert panel on a quarterly basis to benchmark internal scoring and reduce drift.
review: Core tension: achieving high inter-rater reliability while preserving timely analysis; Weakness: external benchmarking may not align with local clinical practices and adds logistical overhead.

name: Data governance and sharing scope for rich physiologic datasets
consequences: A robust data governance framework protects privacy and enables controlled external access. It also creates friction that may slow replication and cross-site validation if processes become too opaque or burdensome.
options: Establish a tiered data access model with role-based permissions and a data use agreement workflow for external researchers.
Tag datasets with provenance metadata and enforce an embargo period before public release to balance verification and privacy.
Set up automated de-identification pipelines and audit trails to satisfy institutional and legal data protection requirements.
review: Core tension: privacy protection vs data accessibility; Weakness: overly stringent controls may delay external validation and multi-site replication, reducing impact.

name: Operational readiness for scalable expansion and capital planning
consequences: A staged expansion plan ties capacity growth to data quality and safety milestones, enabling disciplined scaling. The trade-off is that ambitious gating criteria may slow progress if milestones prove harder to meet than anticipated.
options: Develop a staged expansion plan tied to predefined data quality and safety metrics, enabling gradual growth without overextending resources.
Pilot a modular expansion with independent subunits and shared services to test scalability while preserving core standards.
Secure contingency funding and flexible staffing contracts to absorb surges in participant enrollment without compromising safety.
review: Core tension: growth ambition versus quality safeguards; Weakness: gating criteria may be gamed or fail to capture unanticipated risks, delaying progress.

name: Phased integration of semi-automated event-triage tools
consequences: Semi-automated triage reduces manual review burden and accelerates insights, enabling faster iteration of scoring protocols. The trade-off is potential overreliance on algorithms that may misclassify ambiguous events if not properly supervised.
options: Prototype semi-automated triage on retrospective datasets, with human-in-the-loop adjudication before any live deployment.
Roll out event-triage tools across nights with an opt-in pilot per participant, evaluating sensitivity and false-positive rates before full adoption.
Develop a parallel track of algorithmic benchmarking against dual human scoring to document performance and prevent premature deployment.
review: Core tension: efficiency gains vs accuracy and clinician trust; Weakness: retrospective benchmarking may not reveal edge-case performance on rare parasomnia morphologies.

name: Nightly monitoring intensity aligned to event yield through adaptive scheduling
consequences: Adjust the mix of low-burden nights versus enhanced PSG nights to reflect observed event yield, reducing unnecessary full-night studies while maintaining data depth for phenotype discovery. Trade-offs include potential delays in capturing rare episodes if escalation thresholds are overly cautious, potentially reducing study power.
options: Increase enhanced PSG intensity only after a predefined event yield threshold is met for a given cohort, then taper as yield stabilizes.
Implement rolling eligibility rules for enhanced nights based on ongoing scoring performance to preserve data quality while limiting overuse.
Schedule adaptive nights based on interim reviews by the scoring team to balance safety margins with operational efficiency.
review: Core tension: balancing safety and data yield with resource constraints. Weakness: none of the options specify a rapid reallocation trigger for sudden yield shifts or participant variability.

name: Tiered sensing architecture with escalation-triggered full PSG
consequences: Institute a tiered model where routine nights rely on dry-electrode and contact-free sensors, with escalation to full PSG when flagged events occur; this lowers cost and participant burden while preserving diagnostic depth. Downstream: reliance on sensor thresholds risks missing atypical presentations if criteria are not well-tuned, potentially biasing event capture toward obvious episodes.
options: Deploy escalation triggers using deterministic thresholding on sensor data to automatically flag episodes for full PSG reviews.
Institute a blinded second-party review for escalation decisions to reduce false positives and protect participant privacy.
Centralize escalation decisions into a monitoring hub to reduce response times and ensure consistent protocol adherence.
review: Core tension: dependence on sensor thresholds to trigger escalation may miss atypical episodes. Weakness: options lack a strategy to guard against false negatives from overly conservative thresholds.

name: Milestone-driven facility expansion conditional on data quality and safety metrics
consequences: Expansion only proceeds after meeting predefined data quality and safety KPIs, ensuring sustainable operations; downstream, the cautious pace may slow overall research outputs and funding progress if milestones prove slow, risking delays in scale-up.
options: Proceed with physical expansion exclusively after completion of a pilot cohort demonstrating data quality targets and safe staffing ratios.
Adopt a staged lease extension with optional second-half build-out contingent on achieving publication milestones and data completeness.
Lock in flexible renovation agreements allowing rapid reconfiguration to support increased suite capacity when metrics meet acceptance criteria.
review: Core tension: timing expansion against proven data quality and safety; Weakness: no explicit contingency criteria if metrics are borderline or ambiguous.

name: Privacy-forward video governance with redaction and controlled access
consequences: Establish privacy-preserving video handling enabling adjudication without exposing identifying features; downstream, this adds governance complexity and potentially slows data sharing for external validation.
options: Implement a redaction pipeline that strips identifiable features from video streams before storage and review.
Establish role-based access controls and audit trails to limit video visibility to authorized scorers and external reviewers.
Pursue selective sharing agreements with approved researchers under strict data-use agreements to balance openness and privacy.
review: Core tension: privacy constraints limit data usefulness; Weakness: no explicit plan for third-party access under ethical frameworks or for differential privacy.

name: Recruitment funnel optimization through regional clinician partnerships and streamlined pre-admission pathway
consequences: Streamlines recruitment and improves enrollment efficiency; downstream, reliance on partner channels may bias sample composition if referral pathways skew demographics or eligibility.
options: Forge formal partnerships with regional sleep clinics to pre-screen and refer eligible participants, expediting consent and screening.
Implement centralized pre-admission checks with standardized collateral history to reduce screening variability and time-to-enrollment.
Offer transportation stipends and flexible scheduling to reduce barriers for eligible participants and maintain enrollment targets.
review: Core tension: fast recruitment vs representativeness; Weakness: no explicit mechanism to monitor sample diversity or manage referral bias.

name: Independent adjudication workflow integration with centralized scorer arbitration
consequences: Consolidates scoring for higher reliability; downstream, bottlenecks may emerge if scorer capacity is insufficient or arbitration rules become overly rigid, potentially delaying event adjudication.
options: Create a centralized adjudication hub with parallel scoring teams to balance throughput and consensus.
Implement a double-blind review protocol with predefined adjudication rules to minimize bias and accelerate decisions.
Schedule weekly calibration sessions among scorers to harmonize criteria and improve inter-rater reliability.
review: Core tension: reliability vs speed in adjudication; Weakness: no plan for backlog management or cross-site arbitration capacity.
